summary_text: 1) **Hard constraints for drafting-only mode** - Posting mode must remain `draft` unless explicit scheduling or immediate publish is requested. - For this Forbes URL, `docs/x-auto-note-linking-rule.md` does **not** apply (it is only for `note.com` lead posts). - If treated as quote/reply flow, body must not include `x.com`/`twitter.com`; reference URL goes in `Source URLs`. - Notion is the state hub for `Body`, `Body JA`, `Source URLs`, `Image`, `Status`, `Tweet ID` (any row changes should stay synced there). - Schedulable draft target is `800-1000字` unless explicitly marked as non-posting ideation. - Publish-facing text/image must avoid person/company/product/brand names unless explicitly approved by user. - Proper-noun leakage check is required before sync/post (`Body`, `Body JA`, thumbnail prompt/overlay). 2) **Required preflight items for a new draft from an external article** - Classify task mode: `draft` (now), and confirm no implicit publish/schedule intent. - Confirm schedule carry-over (if any prior slot like `22:00` exists). - Confirm output language scope: English / Japanese / bilingual (`Body` + `Body JA`). - Confirm URL handling mode: - external article canonical URL in `Source URLs`, - if quote/reply flow, no X URL in body. - Confirm Notion target: new row vs existing row update. - Confirm body length intent: schedulable `800-1000字` or short ideation stub. - Run proper-noun leakage check and decide rewrite vs explicit approval. - Confirm thumbnail policy (image required now or text-only draft). - Batch hygiene check: avoid repeating same pillar/pattern concentration. 3) **Repo-specific blockers** - **Mode ambiguity blocker**: external Forbes article is not auto-mapped to `note.com` lead-post rule; quote/reply vs general draft handling must be explicitly chosen. - **Proper-noun policy blocker**: source likely contains branded entities; without explicit approval, body/overlay must be generalized. - **Operational target blocker**: not enough instruction yet on `Body` vs `Body JA`, Notion row destination (new/update), and whether this draft must be immediately schedulable.
